framing: Management accountant at Unilever transitioning to sustainability analyst role at climate tech startup in Paris

jd.title: Sustainability Analyst
jd.style_note: Vague/culture-first: mission-driven, light on specifics, values passion and self-direction

requirements.text: You care deeply about climate action and want your work to make a measurable difference
You have 3+ years of experience working with data — we don't care if that's in sustainability, finance, consulting, or engineering
You're comfortable building models in Excel/Google Sheets and picking up new tools quickly
You can communicate complex topics to non-technical stakeholders clearly and concisely
Bonus: you know GHG Protocol, CSRD, or carbon accounting fundamentals
Bonus: you've worked with sustainability reporting frameworks (GRI, SASB, TCFD, or ISSB/IFRS S1-S2)
Bonus: Python or SQL skills for data analysis

responsibilities: Help enterprise customers measure, track, and report their carbon emissions using Sweep's platform
Build custom carbon accounting models for complex supply chains (Scope 1, 2, and 3)
Onboard new customers and train their sustainability teams on carbon measurement methodology
Analyse customer emissions data for accuracy and completeness before external reporting
Stay current with evolving regulations (EU CSRD, SEC climate disclosure, ISSB standards)

expected_output.summary: Management accountant with 6 years at Unilever Netherlands, most recently in the sustainability reporting team supporting Unilever's annual Climate Transition Action Plan disclosure. Previously in group management accounting handling P&L consolidation for Benelux markets. Volunteered for Unilever's internal carbon footprint measurement pilot project (Scope 1 and 2). CIMA qualified. Strong Excel modelling and financial reporting skills. Seeking full transition to climate/sustainability career.

experience.title: Management Accountant — Sustainability Reporting
bullets: Supported preparation of Unilever's annual Climate Transition Action Plan (CTAP) and TCFD-aligned disclosures
Built Excel models consolidating Scope 1 and Scope 2 emissions data from 15 Benelux manufacturing sites
Reconciled energy consumption data (natural gas, electricity, diesel) with financial records for GHG calculations
Collaborated with external auditors (PwC) on limited assurance engagement for Unilever's sustainability report
Trained 8 site finance managers on emissions data collection templates and GHG Protocol methodology

experience.title: Management Accountant — Group Reporting (promoted from Graduate Trainee, 2022)
bullets: Performed monthly P&L consolidation for Unilever Benelux markets (EUR 2.1B revenue)
Prepared variance analysis and management commentary for quarterly business reviews
Built financial models for brand profitability analysis across Home Care and Personal Care divisions
Completed CIMA qualification while working full-time (2020-2022)

certifications: CIMA — Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (2022)
GHG Protocol Corporate Standard — Self-study completion (2023)
education: BSc Business Economics — University of Amsterdam (2016-2020)

jd_requirements: 3+ years working with data sustainability finance consulting engineering
Excel Google Sheets models new tools
GRI SASB TCFD ISSB IFRS S1-S2 sustainability reporting
deliberate_flaws: No Scope 3 emissions experience — only Scope 1 and 2 (Sweep's core value prop is Scope 3 supply chain)
No CSRD experience (EU directive is new, Unilever was reporting under earlier frameworks)
No ISSB/IFRS S1-S2 experience (new standards, not yet applied at Unilever during her tenure)
No GRI or SASB direct experience (contributed to TCFD only)
No Python or SQL skills — all Excel-based
No carbon accounting platform experience (Sweep, Watershed, Sweep, etc.)
No customer-facing experience — internal accounting role, not client-facing
No startup experience — Unilever is large corporate, different pace and structure
Career changer — accounting to sustainability, no formal sustainability qualification beyond self-study
GHG Protocol knowledge is self-study, not formal training or certification
No supply chain carbon modelling experience (financial supply chain, not emissions supply chain)

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
